zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick於大約1206年出生在英國蘇格蘭藍納可爾克勞福德時，她的父親Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland38歲，母親Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus33歲。她於1234年與Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick在蘇格蘭艾斯爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1255年在英國蘇格蘭吉德布萊次爾享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick于大约1206年出生在英国苏格兰藍納可爾克勞福德时，她的父亲Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland38岁，母亲Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus33岁。她于1234年与Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick在苏格兰艾斯爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1255年在英国苏格兰吉德布萊次爾享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick was born about 1206, in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland, was 38 and her mother, Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus, was 33. She married Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick in 1234, in Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1255, in Kirkcudbrightshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick est née environ 1206, à Crawford, Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick en 1234, à Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1255, à Kirkcudbrightshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick um 1206 in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland 38 und ihre Mutter Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus 33. Sie hat 1234 in Ayrshire, Scotland Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1255 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Kirkcudbrightshire, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick è nata il circa 1206 nel Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick nel 1234, nel Ayrshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1255, nel Kirkcudbrightshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrickが約1206年にイギリススコットランドラナルクシュアイアクラウフォードで生まれた時，父Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotlandは38歳，母Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angusは33歳であった。1234年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアでNiall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrickと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1255年にイギリススコットランドキルククウドブリトシュアイアで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick(이)가 대략 1206년 영국 스코틀랜드 랜아르크실르 크롤퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1234년 스컷랜드 에이르실르에서 Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1255년 영국 스코틀랜드 커크쿠드브라이츠히르에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick nasceu aproximadamente 1206, em Crawford, Lanarkshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick em 1234, em Ayrshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1255, em Kirkcudbrightshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick родилась около 1206 г. в местности кравфорд, ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick в 1234 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1255 г. в местности кирккудбраицхир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick nació aproximadamente 1206, en Crawford, Lanarkshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick en 1234, en Ayrshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1255, en Kirkcudbrightshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 50 años.
